prevalence.source: IHME GBD (tension-type headache) + national survey context
prevalence.trend: stable; high underreporting in primary care

incidence.value: high (recurrent episodic); ~50-150 per 1,000/year reporting clinically significant episodes

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: ICHD-3: bilateral pressing/tightening headache, mild-moderate intensity, not aggravated by routine activity; no nausea/vomiting; may have photophobia or phonophobia (not both)

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: tens of millions with episodic symptoms
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate with lifestyle and appropriate analgesic use; medication overuse risk exists

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often diagnosed clinically in APS; red flag screening to exclude secondary causes
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Education, stress management, sleep hygiene, NSAIDs/analgesics as needed; preventive strategies (e.g., amitriptyline) for chronic forms
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Medication overuse headache and limited access to non-pharmacologic therapies
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: APS can deliver lifestyle counseling and manage comorbid anxiety/insomnia
